---
title: "SEM"
output: html_document
---

STRUCTURAL EQUATION MODELLING

```{r}
library(here)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
library(MASS)
library(piecewiseSEM)
library(dplyr)
library(glmmTMB)
library(semEff)
```

```{r}
results <- read.csv(here("data", "clean data", "results.csv"), colClasses = c(sex = "character"))

#make maternal ID a factor
results$maternalID <- as.factor(results$maternalID)

#remove ambient, because these need to be modelled separately. also remove larval treatment of cool and mean, because the sample size isn't big enough to model - just not enough clutches.
#I think I will model the ambient larva treatment separately.
results_filtered <- results %>%
  filter(
    larval_treatment %in% c("warm", "hot"),
    pupal_treatment != "ambient", clutch_size != 0) %>%
  mutate(numerical_pupal_treatment = case_when(
    pupal_treatment == "cool"  ~ 10.07,
    pupal_treatment == "mean"  ~  12.08,
    pupal_treatment == "warm"  ~  14.08,
    pupal_treatment == "hot"   ~  15.54,
  )) %>% 
  mutate(numerical_larval_treatment = case_when(
    larval_treatment == "warm"  ~  12.76,
    larval_treatment == "hot"   ~  13.89,
  )) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg = weight * 1000) %>%
  mutate(weight_mg_centered = weight_mg - mean(weight_mg, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(across(
    c(weight_mg, egg_mean_1A, clutch_size),
    ~ as.numeric(scale(.)),
    .names = "{.col}_z"
  ))


```

```{r}
#filter the dataset to just have the columns I need
sem_data <- results_filtered %>%
  dplyr::select(adultID, weight_mg, clutch_size, egg_mean_1A,
                numerical_larval_treatment, numerical_pupal_treatment,
                maternalID) %>%
  filter(!is.na(egg_mean_1A))
```



```{r}
# individual models to go into the psem
m_model <- lm(weight_mg ~ numerical_larval_treatment + numerical_pupal_treatment,
              data = sem_data)

y_model <- glm.nb(clutch_size ~ numerical_larval_treatment + numerical_pupal_treatment + weight_mg, data = sem_data)

x_model <- lmer(egg_mean_1A ~ numerical_pupal_treatment + numerical_larval_treatment + clutch_size + weight_mg + (1|maternalID), data = sem_data)

# SEM with correlated error between clutch size and egg size
sem_model <- psem(
  m_model,
  y_model,
  x_model,
  clutch_size %~~% egg_mean_1A   # this adds the correlated residual
)


# Summary with standardized coefficients
summary(sem_model, standardize = "scale",  conserve = TRUE) 

#standardised coefficients
coefs(sem_model, standardize = "scale", standardize.type = "Menard.OE")

# Plot standardized SEM
plot(sem_model, show = "std", digits = 2)




```


```{r}
#use the semEff package to get standardised effects and confidence intervals
#set.seed(123)
#standardised_effects <- semEff(sem_model,R = 1000, ran.eff = "maternalID")

standardised_effects <- readRDS("~/University stuff/Year 4/results/R project/data/clean data/standardised_effects.rds")


summary(standardised_effects)

print(standardised_effects)

#save the standardised effects so I don't have to re-run the bootstrap every time
#saveRDS(
# standardised_effects,
 # file = here("data", "clean data", "standardised_effects.rds")
#)
```



```{r}
#to get unstandardised (raw) coefficients
#set.seed(123)

#effects <- bootEff(sem_model, R = 1000, ran.eff = "maternalID", incl.raw = TRUE) 

#raw_effects <- semEff(effects, use.raw = TRUE)

#saveRDS(raw_effects, file = here("data", "clean data", "raw_effects.rds"))


raw_effects <- readRDS("~/University stuff/Year 4/results/R project/data/clean data/raw_effects.rds")


summary(raw_effects)


```

now I want to generate model predictions so that I can plot them
```{r}
sem.boot <- bootEff(sem_model, R = 1000, seed = 123, ran.eff = "maternalID")

sem.eff <- semEff(sem.boot)

tot <- getTotEff(sem.eff, "numerical_larval_treatment")

#i want to plot the total effect of larval temp on clutch size

fit <- sapply("numerical_larval_treatment", function(i) {

  x <- data.frame(seq(min(sem_data[[i]]), max(sem_data[[i]]), length = 100))
  names(x) <- i
  
  f <- predEff(sem_model,
               newdata = x,
               effects = tot[i],
               eff.boot = tot.b)
  
  cbind(x, f)

}, simplify = FALSE)
```
